concept: State / Relevant Conditions of a System at a Time
description: The set of properties or conditions selected to describe a component or system at a particular time. A state may be observed, inferred, measured, or assigned by a model, and the selected variables must be stated when ambiguity matters. The word does not mean a government, territory, emotional condition, moral status, or every fact about the thing.
sound_symbolism: Grounded 'no' opens a settled snapshot, 'mi' gathers several conditions, and 'rela' lays them out as a relation visible for comparison. The open ending leaves room for the state to change.
grammatical_notes: A noun commonly possessed by the system or component described: 'ha terulora nomirela mureo nai.' (This system's state is stable.) Use time expressions and evidentials to identify when and how the state is known. Distinct from 'norelu' (form), 'noa' (position), and a temporary adjective used without an analytical state description.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Tracking system states supports transparent maintenance and adaptation while exposing which conditions a design chooses to notice.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A state is presented as a conditioned description at a time, not as a permanent essence.

tags.cognition: a selected description used to analyze change
tags.physical: the term can describe measurable material and operational conditions
tags.temporal: conditions attributed to a particular time
